title: Survive on nothing but Eva branded food drink for a week
display_title: Survive on nothing but Eva branded food drink for a week
wikitext:
[]: Survive on nothing but Eva-branded food/drink for a week?             Quote Post by AR-99 » Thu Aug 23, 2012 4:17 pm Pipe dream or possibility, just not very healthy? This is a hypothetical based on the premise that one is in Japan to do this. My understanding is that Lawson's (still?) has a lot of Eva-branded food and drinks but it's largely snacks and the like. Or there are other places that would enable one to achieve this goal by getting other means of sustenance?

[]:             Quote Post by Murmur » Thu Aug 23, 2012 4:52 pm When I joined that forum, I expected walls of text in a typical Madoka-like style. But then I got a thread about surviving on Evangelion products alone. And then I said to myself "oh ho ho, what the heck", and I couldn't decide if it was insanity or pure genius. So anyway, I'll be watching this thread, because you got me interested.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Thu Aug 23, 2012 4:52 pm if you survive on coffee already just load up on the UCC!

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Aug 23, 2012 6:07 pm The hotel near Fuji-Q where the Eva-themed hotel room is at serves Eva themed food, which is pretty normal stuff, like Pasta. You can easily survive off of that for a week. Oh here it is: http://www.evageeks.org/2010/07/random-evangelion-themed-lunches/

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Thu Aug 23, 2012 11:17 pm Hmm, the lunch link is dead. :( I was not as specific as I could be. What I had in mind was being in an urban area like Tokyo, eating nothing Eva-branded or themed sustenance, and having some variety. Different flavor of ramen for all 3 meals?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Aug 24, 2012 1:50 am AR-99 wrote: Hmm, the lunch link is dead. :( I was not as specific as I could be. What I had in mind was being in an urban area like Tokyo, eating nothing Eva-branded or themed sustenance, and having some variety. Different flavor of ramen for all 3 meals?

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Sat Aug 25, 2012 12:59 pm You know what we need to put together? The Evangelion Cookbook .
